free_text: Isomeric ratios of the cross sections for
Sn(p,xpyn)X reactions
an English
on Russian
The previous investigations of proton reactions on tin
isotopes.
Decay data
-By authors. The ratio of the 18 isomeric pairs as a
function of the energy of protons and the number of the
emitted nucleons was investigated.  The isotope effect
and the cross sections of the charge-exchange reactions
were discussed.  The experimental values of the cross
sections of reactions (p,xpyn) were compared with the
theoretical ones calculated by cascade-evaporation
model.
-By compiler. Original paper contains some incorrect
spin-parity values. Error index see:
state         original          ENSDF
compiler saved original values of spin-parities, but
an user must take in attention the information.
Gamma spectra measurements continued during to
one year.
For 1 GeV
For 8.1 GeV
The uncertainty is maximal value of the
difference between average value and cross sections of
separate measurements.
The uncertainty includes statistical ones for detector
efficiency, beam intensity and target thickness.
Tbl.1,2 from Yad.Fiz.,65,810,2002
same in Phys.At.Nucl.,65,776,2002
SD: ref. on translation added.
SAMPLES was added to Subents 2-79.
-SB- Correction was made according
Memo CP-D/797
SD: Corrections in several Subents.
Parity and spin of the product
-By compiler:
1. Ru-97 production cross section is shown as
independent, but production cross section of Rh-97,
which is progenitor of Ru-97, was not measured.
2. Tc-93-g production cross section is shown as
independent, but production cross section of Ru-93,
which is progenitor of Tc-93-g, was not measured.
3. Mn-52 production cross section is shown as
independent, but production cross section of Fe-52,
which is progenitor of Mn-52, was not measured.
Isomer 0 <-> 1 for Tc-94 was replaced.
SD: SF5=IND deleted from REACTION code.
